member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Application
member.summary: Provides global application state flags.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Application.IsPlaying
member.summary: True when the game is actively running (play mode in editor, or standalone player).

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Application.IsEditor
member.summary: True when running inside the editor (false in standalone builds).

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Application.IsPaused
member.summary: True when play mode is paused. Update/FixedUpdate/LateUpdate stop, but rendering continues.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Application.StepRequested
member.summary: When true, one frame of gameplay executes then IsPaused reasserts. Set by the editor Step button, consumed by the game loop.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Application.ShouldRunGameplay
member.summary: Whether gameplay should execute this frame (playing and not paused, or stepping).

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Application.IsGameplayExecuting
member.summary: True while gameplay code (Update/FixedUpdate) is executing. Used by the editor's input filtering to distinguish gameplay input from editor input.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Application.DataPath
member.summary: Directory containing the running executable (standalone) or project root (editor). Used by PlayerAssetDatabase to locate assets relative to the executable.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.AssetRef`1
member.summary: A serializable reference to an asset. Stores a GUID for persistent identification and caches the resolved instance. When the asset is needed, it's loaded via AssetDatabase.Get(). This solves the "stale reference" problem — all references to the same asset share the same resolved instance, and re-resolving always gets the latest version.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.AssetRef`1.Res
member.summary: The resolved asset. Lazily loaded via AssetDatabase.Get() if not cached. Returns null if the asset is missing or this ref is explicitly null.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.AssetRef`1.ResWeak
member.summary: Returns the cached instance without attempting to load. May be null.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.AssetRef`1.AssetID
member.summary: The asset GUID. Prefers the live instance's AssetID when available.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.AssetRef`1.EnsureLoaded
member.summary: Force a reload from the asset database.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.AssetRef`1.Detach
member.summary: Clear the cached instance. Next access will re-resolve from the database.

member.summary: Gets the normalized lifetime (0 to 1) of the particle.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.ParticleSystem.Particle.IsAlive
member.summary: Returns true if the particle is still alive.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.ParticleSystem.ParticleSystemComponent
member.summary: A GPU-instanced particle system component. Particles are rendered using instanced rendering for optimal performance.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.ParticleSystem.ParticleSystemModule
member.summary: Base class for all particle system modules. Modules control different aspects of particle behavior.

member.summary: Called when particles are spawned to initialize their values.

member.summary: Called every frame to update particle values.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController
member.summary: A character controller that handles collision detection and movement. provides just the core functionality.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.ColliderShape
member.summary: The shape type used for the character controller collision detection.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.MaxSlopeAngle
member.summary: Maximum angle in degrees for a surface to be considered walkable (default: 45 degrees)

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.SnapDownDistance
member.summary: Distance to snap down to ground when walking off slopes (default: 0.5)

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.StepSize
member.summary: Maximum height the character can step up onto (default: 0.3)

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.IsGrounded
member.summary: Whether the character controller is currently grounded.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.Move(Prowl.Vector.Float3)
member.summary: Moves the character controller by the specified motion vector. This handles collision detection and sliding. Also updates the IsGrounded state.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.UpdateGroundedState(Prowl.Vector.Float3)
member.summary: Updates the grounded state by performing a ground check.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.PerformGroundCheck(Prowl.Vector.Float3,System.Single,Prowl.Runtime.ShapeCastHit@)
member.summary: Performs a ground check using shape casting. Only considers the character grounded if the surface angle is walkable.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.TrySetHeight(System.Single)
member.summary: Attempts to set the height of the collider. Returns true if successful, false if the new size would collide with something.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.TrySetRadius(System.Single)
member.summary: Attempts to set the radius of the collider. Returns true if successful, false if the new size would collide with something.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.CheckShapeOverlap(Prowl.Vector.Float3,System.Single,System.Single)
member.summary: Checks if a shape with the given dimensions would overlap with anything.

member.summary: Performs a shape cast based on the current shape type.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.TryStepUp(Prowl.Vector.Float3,Prowl.Vector.Float3,System.Single,Prowl.Vector.Float3@)
member.summary: Attempts to step up onto an obstacle. Returns true if step-up was successful, with the new position.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.CharacterController.GetSlopeAngle(Prowl.Vector.Float3)
member.summary: Calculates the angle of a surface in degrees from horizontal.

member.summary: Snaps the character down to the ground when walking on slopes. This prevents the character from "floating" when transitioning between slopes.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.BoxCollider.Size
member.summary: Gets or sets the dimensions of the box.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Collider._attachedBody
member.summary: The Jitter2 rigidbody this collider is currently attached to. This could be either a Rigidbody3D's body or the PhysicsWorld's static rigidbody.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Collider._attachedRigidbody3D
member.summary: The Rigidbody3D component this collider is attached to, if any. Null if attached to the static rigidbody.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Collider._attachedShapes
member.summary: The shapes created by this collider and added to the rigidbody.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Collider._lastTransformVersion
member.summary: Transform version tracking for static colliders. Used to detect when the transform has moved and shapes need updating.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Collider._lastLayer
member.summary: Layer tracking for static colliders. Used to detect when the layer has changed and we need to move to a different static rigidbody.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Collider.IsClaimed
member.summary: Returns true if this collider is already attached to a rigidbody. Used to prevent multiple rigidbodies from claiming the same collider.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Collider.TryAttachTo(Prowl.Runtime.Rigidbody3D)
member.summary: Attempts to attach this collider to a Rigidbody3D. Returns false if the collider is already claimed by another rigidbody.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Collider.AttachToStatic
member.summary: Attaches this collider to the static rigidbody in the physics world. Uses the GameObject's layer to determine which static rigidbody to attach to.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Collider.Detach
member.summary: Detaches this collider from its current rigidbody.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Collider.RegisterShapes
member.summary: Registers this collider's shapes with its attached rigidbody.

member.summary: Create the Jitter Physics RigidBodyShape

member.summary: Create the Transformed Jitter Physics RigidBodyShape

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Collider.CreateWorldTransformedShapes
member.summary: Create shapes transformed into world space for the static rigidbody.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.ModelCollider
member.summary: Creates a triangle mesh collider from a Mesh asset.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.AngularMotorConstraint
member.summary: A motor constraint that drives relative angular movement between two axes fixed in the reference frames of the bodies. Useful for creating powered hinges, rotating platforms, and wheels.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.AngularMotorConstraint.Axis1
member.summary: The motor axis in local space of the first rigidbody.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.AngularMotorConstraint.Axis2
member.summary: The motor axis in local space of the second rigidbody. If no connected body is specified, this is in world space.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.AngularMotorConstraint.TargetVelocity
member.summary: Target angular velocity for the motor in radians per second. Positive values rotate in the direction of the axis.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.AngularMotorConstraint.MaximumForce
member.summary: Maximum torque the motor can apply. Set to 0 to disable the motor.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.AngularMotorConstraint.LocalAxis1
member.summary: Gets the local axis on the first body.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.AngularMotorConstraint.LocalAxis2
member.summary: Gets the local axis on the second body.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.BallSocketConstraint
member.summary: A ball-and-socket constraint that connects two rigidbodies at a point, allowing free rotation but constraining translation. Also known as a point-to-point constraint or spherical joint.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.BallSocketConstraint.Anchor
member.summary: The anchor point in local space of this rigidbody.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.BallSocketConstraint.Softness
member.summary: Softness of the constraint. Higher values make the constraint softer.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.BallSocketConstraint.BiasFactor
member.summary: Bias factor for error correction. Higher values correct errors faster.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.BallSocketConstraint.Impulse
member.summary: Gets the accumulated impulse applied by this constraint.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.ConeLimitConstraint
member.summary: Restricts the tilt of one body relative to another body within a cone shape. Useful for creating ball-and-socket joints with angular limits (ragdoll joints).

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.ConeLimitConstraint.Axis
member.summary: The cone axis in local space of this rigidbody.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.ConeLimitConstraint.MinAngle
member.summary: Minimum cone angle in degrees. Default is 0.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.ConeLimitConstraint.MaxAngle
member.summary: Maximum cone angle in degrees. Default is 45. This defines the cone's opening angle from the axis.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.ConeLimitConstraint.Softness
member.summary: Softness of the constraint. Higher values make the constraint softer.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.ConeLimitConstraint.BiasFactor
member.summary: Bias factor for error correction. Higher values correct errors faster.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.ConeLimitConstraint.Angle
member.summary: Gets the current angle between the axes in degrees.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.ConeLimitConstraint.Impulse
member.summary: Gets the accumulated impulse applied by this constraint.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.DistanceLimitConstraint
member.summary: Constrains the distance between two anchor points on two rigidbodies. Can be used to create rope-like connections or maintain a specific distance.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.DistanceLimitConstraint.Anchor
member.summary: The anchor point in local space of this rigidbody.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.DistanceLimitConstraint.ConnectedAnchor
member.summary: The anchor point in local space of the connected rigidbody.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.DistanceLimitConstraint.TargetDistance
member.summary: The target distance to maintain between the anchors.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.DistanceLimitConstraint.MinDistance
member.summary: Minimum allowed distance. Use float.NegativeInfinity for no minimum.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.DistanceLimitConstraint.MaxDistance
member.summary: Maximum allowed distance. Use float.PositiveInfinity for no maximum.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.DistanceLimitConstraint.Softness
member.summary: Softness of the constraint. Higher values make the constraint softer.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.DistanceLimitConstraint.BiasFactor
member.summary: Bias factor for error correction. Higher values correct errors faster.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.DistanceLimitConstraint.CurrentDistance
member.summary: Gets the current distance between the anchors.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.DistanceLimitConstraint.Impulse
member.summary: Gets the accumulated impulse applied by this constraint.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.FixedAngleConstraint
member.summary: Constrains the relative orientation between two rigidbodies, preventing all rotation. Also known as a weld joint (when combined with position constraints).

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.FixedAngleConstraint.Softness
member.summary: Softness of the constraint. Higher values make the constraint softer.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.FixedAngleConstraint.BiasFactor
member.summary: Bias factor for error correction. Higher values correct errors faster.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.FixedAngleConstraint.Impulse
member.summary: Gets the accumulated impulse applied by this constraint.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.HingeAngleConstraint
member.summary: Constrains two bodies to only allow rotation around a specified axis, removing two angular degrees of freedom. A hinge joint that can optionally enforce angle limits.

member.summary: The hinge axis in local space of this rigidbody.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.HingeAngleConstraint.MinAngle
member.summary: Minimum angle in degrees. Default is -180.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.HingeAngleConstraint.MaxAngle
member.summary: Maximum angle in degrees. Default is 180.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.HingeAngleConstraint.Softness
member.summary: Softness of the constraint. Higher values make the constraint softer.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.HingeAngleConstraint.LimitSoftness
member.summary: Softness of the angle limit. Higher values make the limit softer.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.HingeAngleConstraint.BiasFactor
member.summary: Bias factor for error correction. Higher values correct errors faster.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.HingeAngleConstraint.LimitBias
member.summary: Bias factor for limit error correction.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.HingeAngleConstraint.Angle
member.summary: Gets the current hinge angle in degrees.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.HingeAngleConstraint.Impulse
member.summary: Gets the accumulated impulse applied by this constraint.

member.summary: A hinge joint that constrains two bodies to rotate around a shared axis. Similar to a door hinge. Composed of HingeAngle and BallSocket constraints.

member.summary: The anchor point in local space where the joint connects.

member.summary: The axis of rotation in local space.

member.summary: Maximum angle limit in degrees.

member.summary: Whether this joint has a motor attached.

member.summary: Target velocity for the motor (if enabled).

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.HingeJoint.MotorMaxForce
member.summary: Maximum force the motor can apply (if enabled).

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.HingeJoint.CurrentAngleDegrees
member.summary: Gets the current angle of the hinge in degrees.

member.summary: A motor constraint that drives relative translational movement along two axes fixed in the reference frames of the bodies. Useful for creating powered sliders and linear actuators.

member.summary: The motor axis in local space of the first rigidbody.

member.summary: Target velocity for the motor. Positive values move along the axis direction.

member.summary: Maximum force the motor can apply. Set to 0 to disable the motor.

member.summary: Gets the underlying Jitter2 joint.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnLineConstraint
member.summary: Constrains a fixed point on one body to a line that is fixed on another body. This constraint removes one or two degrees of translational freedom depending on the limit. Useful for creating slider joints with rails.

member.summary: The line axis in local space of the first rigidbody.

member.summary: Anchor point on the first body that defines the line.

member.summary: Anchor point on the second body that is constrained to the line.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnLineConstraint.MinDistance
member.summary: Minimum allowed distance along the line axis. Use float.NegativeInfinity for no minimum.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnLineConstraint.MaxDistance
member.summary: Maximum allowed distance along the line axis. Use float.PositiveInfinity for no maximum.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnLineConstraint.Softness
member.summary: Softness of the constraint. Higher values make the constraint softer.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnLineConstraint.LimitSoftness
member.summary: Softness of the distance limit. Higher values make the limit softer.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnLineConstraint.BiasFactor
member.summary: Bias factor for error correction. Higher values correct errors faster.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnLineConstraint.LimitBias
member.summary: Bias factor for limit error correction.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnLineConstraint.Distance
member.summary: Gets the current distance along the line axis.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnLineConstraint.Impulse
member.summary: Gets the accumulated impulse applied by this constraint.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnPlaneConstraint
member.summary: Constrains a fixed point on one body to a plane that is fixed on another body. This constraint removes one degree of translational freedom if the limit is enforced. Useful for creating sliding surfaces or limiting movement to a plane.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnPlaneConstraint.PlaneNormal
member.summary: The plane normal in local space of the first rigidbody.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnPlaneConstraint.Anchor1
member.summary: Anchor point on the first body that defines the plane position.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnPlaneConstraint.Anchor2
member.summary: Anchor point on the second body that is constrained to the plane.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnPlaneConstraint.MinDistance
member.summary: Minimum allowed distance from the plane. Use float.NegativeInfinity for no minimum.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnPlaneConstraint.MaxDistance
member.summary: Maximum allowed distance from the plane. Use float.PositiveInfinity for no maximum.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnPlaneConstraint.Softness
member.summary: Softness of the constraint. Higher values make the constraint softer.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnPlaneConstraint.BiasFactor
member.summary: Bias factor for error correction. Higher values correct errors faster.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PointOnPlaneConstraint.Impulse
member.summary: Gets the accumulated impulse applied by this constraint.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.PrismaticJoint
member.summary: A prismatic (slider) joint that constrains two bodies to move along a single axis. Like a piston or drawer slide. Composed of PointOnLine with optional angle constraints and motor.

member.summary: The anchor point in local space where the joint connects.

member.summary: The axis of movement in local space.

member.summary: Minimum distance along the axis. Use float.NegativeInfinity for no limit.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PrismaticJoint.MaxDistance
member.summary: Maximum distance along the axis. Use float.PositiveInfinity for no limit.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PrismaticJoint.Pinned

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PrismaticJoint.HasMotor
member.summary: Whether this joint has a motor attached.

member.summary: Target velocity for the motor (if enabled).

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PrismaticJoint.MotorMaxForce
member.summary: Maximum force the motor can apply (if enabled).

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.PrismaticJoint.CurrentDistance
member.summary: Gets the current distance along the slider axis.

member.summary: Bias factor for error correction. Higher values correct errors faster.

member.summary: Gets the current twist angle in degrees.

member.summary: Maximum force the motor can apply (if enabled).

member.summary: Gets the current twist angle in degrees.

member.summary: Gets the world-space height at the specified world position.

member.summary: Gets or sets the steering angle of the wheel in radians.

member.summary: Gets the current angular velocity of the wheel.

member.summary: Gets whether the wheel is currently touching the ground.

member.summary: Gets the current suspension compression (0 = fully extended, 1 = fully compressed).

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.WheelCollider.Radius
member.summary: Gets or sets the wheel radius.

member.summary: Gets or sets the maximum angular velocity of the wheel.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.WheelCollider.Locked

member.summary: Adds drive torque to the wheel.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderer
member.summary: Renders a skinned mesh deformed by bone Transforms. Bones are referenced by path (relative to this GO's root), matching Unity's convention. Paths like "Armature/Hips/Spine" are resolved via Transform.Find(). Supports multiple materials via submeshes. Bone matrices are uploaded via a float texture (no uniform array size limit).

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderer.SharedMesh
member.summary: The mesh to render (may contain submeshes).

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderer.Materials
member.summary: Materials array — one per submesh. If fewer materials than submeshes, last material is reused.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderer.RootBonePath
member.summary: Path to the root bone, relative to this GO's hierarchy root.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderer.BonePaths
member.summary: Paths to all bone Transforms, indexed to match Mesh.BindPoses. Each path is relative to this GO's hierarchy root (e.g. "Armature/Hips/Spine").

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderer.RootBone
member.summary: Get the resolved root bone Transform.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderer.Bones
member.summary: Get the resolved bone Transforms array.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderer.SetBones(Prowl.Vector.Transform[],Prowl.Vector.Transform)
member.summary: Set bone references from live Transforms. Computes paths relative to the hierarchy root. Call this during import when you have the live GO hierarchy.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderer.FindSearchRoot
member.summary: Find the correct search root for bone path resolution. Walks up from this GO to find the topmost ancestor, then tries to resolve. If paths don't resolve from the absolute root (e.g. after reparenting), tries progressively lower ancestors until paths work.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderer.ComputeSkinnedBounds(Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Mesh)
member.summary: Compute world-space AABB from bone positions, padded by the mesh's max vertex extent. Much cheaper than transforming every vertex, and correct for frustum culling.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderer.EnsureBoneTexture(System.Int32)
member.summary: Ensures the bone matrix texture exists and is large enough for the given bone count. Each bone occupies 4 texels (one per matrix row) in a single-row RGBA32F texture.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderer.UploadBoneTexture(Prowl.Vector.Float4x4[])
member.summary: Uploads bone matrices to the texture. Each mat4 is stored as 4 consecutive RGBA32F texels. Layout: texel[bone*4+0] = row0, texel[bone*4+1] = row1, texel[bone*4+2] = row2, texel[bone*4+3] = row3

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Terrain.TerrainChunk
member.summary: Represents a single terrain chunk in the quadtree LOD system. Each chunk is rendered as a GPU instance of the base terrain mesh.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Terrain.TerrainChunk.Subdivide
member.summary: Splits this chunk into 4 children.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Terrain.TerrainChunk.Merge
member.summary: Merges this chunk by removing all children. Converts this node back to a leaf.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Terrain.TerrainChunk.IsLeaf
member.summary: Checks if this chunk is a leaf node (no children).

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Terrain.TerrainComponent
member.summary: GPU-instanced terrain component with quadtree LOD. Renders terrain using a single mesh instanced many times. Heightmap is sampled in the vertex shader for displacement. References a TerrainData asset for height/splat/layer configuration.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Terrain.TerrainComponent.Data
member.summary: The terrain data asset containing heightmap, splatmap, and layer configuration.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Terrain.TerrainComponent.Material
member.summary: Base material. Terrain clones this internally to set its own properties.

member.summary: Maximum LOD subdivision levels for the quadtree.

member.summary: Resolution of the base mesh grid (vertices per side).

member.summary: Grass material override. If null, uses built-in Grass material.

member.summary: Maximum render distance for grass.

member.summary: Global grass density multiplier.

member.summary: Maximum render distance for trees.

member.summary: Invalidate cached grass patches (call after painting grass density).

member.summary: Shortcut to terrain size from the data asset.

member.summary: Shortcut to terrain height from the data asset.

member.summary: Transform a point from terrain-local space to world space.

member.summary: Transform a point from world space to terrain-local space.

member.summary: Gets all visible leaf chunks for rendering.

member.summary: Sort order within the menu. Lower values appear first.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.ExecutionOrderAttribute
member.summary: Indicates the order in which the marked component should execute its update methods in the Prowl Game Engine.
member.remarks: This attribute can only be applied to classes and cannot be used multiple times on the same class.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.HideInInspectorAttribute
member.summary: Hides a field from the inspector but keeps it serialized.

member.summary: Draws a float/int field as a slider with min/max range.

member.summary: Draws a header label above the field in the inspector.

member.summary: Shows a tooltip when hovering the field label.

member.summary: Makes the field read-only in the inspector.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.ButtonAttribute
member.summary: Draws a button in the inspector that calls this method when clicked.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.ShowIfAttribute
member.summary: Only shows the field when the named bool field/property is true.

member.summary: Draws a string field as a multi-line text area.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.AddComponentMenuAttribute
member.summary: Organizes a MonoBehaviour in the Add Component menu. Path uses '/' for categories, e.g. "Physics/Rigidbody".

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.ExecuteAlwaysAttribute
member.summary: When applied to a MonoBehaviour, its gameplay lifecycle methods (Start, Update, LateUpdate, FixedUpdate) are called even when the application is not in play mode (e.g., in the editor). Structural lifecycle methods (OnEnable, OnDisable, OnRenderCollect, etc.) always run regardless.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.GameObject.HierarchyStateChanged
member.summary: Updates the hierarchy state of the GameObject and its children.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.GameObject.IsParentEnabled
member.summary: Checks if the parent of this GameObject is enabled.
member.returns: True if the parent is enabled or if there is no parent, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.GameObject.Print
member.summary: Prints the GameObject hierarchy including all components and children to Debug.Log. Useful for debugging scene structure.

member.summary: Handles a missing component by attempting to recover it.

The serialization context.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.HideFlags
member.summary: Flags that control object destruction, saving, and visibility in the editor.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.LayerMask
member.summary: A struct that represents a layer mask.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour
member.summary: Represents the base class for all scripts that attach to GameObjects in the Prowl Game Engine. MonoBehaviour provides lifecycle methods for game object behaviors.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.HideFlags
member.summary: Gets or sets the hide flags for this MonoBehaviour.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.ShouldExecuteGameplay
member.summary: Whether this component's gameplay methods should execute. True when in play mode, or when the component has [ExecuteAlways].

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.Identifier
member.summary: Gets the identifier for this MonoBehaviour. Generally shouldnt be set manually

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.GameObject
member.summary: Gets the GameObject this MonoBehaviour is attached to.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.Transform
member.summary: Gets the Transform component of the GameObject this MonoBehaviour is attached to.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.HasStarted
member.summary: Gets whether the Start method has been called.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.HasBeenEnabled
member.summary: Gets whether OnEnable has ever been called on this component. Used to determine if OnDispose should be called during cleanup.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.Tag
member.summary: Gets the tag of the GameObject this MonoBehaviour is attached to.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.Enabled
member.summary: Gets or sets whether the MonoBehaviour is enabled.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.EnabledInHierarchy
member.summary: Gets whether the MonoBehaviour is enabled in the hierarchy (considering parent objects).

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.Scene
member.summary:
The parent to which this belongs.
Note that this property is derived from the components , as a itself cannot be part of a without a .

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.CompareTag(System.String)
member.summary: Compares the tag of the GameObject this MonoBehaviour is attached to with the specified tag.
member.param: The tag to compare against.
member.returns: True if the tags match, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.GetSiblingIndex
member.summary: Gets the index of this Component in its GameObject's Component list.
member.returns: The index of this Component in its GameObject's Component list, or null if it has no GameObject.
member.exception: Thrown if the Component is not found in its GameObject's Component list.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.SetSiblingIndex(System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the index of this Component in its GameObject's Component list.
member.param: The new index of this Component.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.AttachToGameObject(Prowl.Runtime.GameObject)
member.summary: Attaches this MonoBehaviour to the specified GameObject.
member.param: The GameObject to attach to.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.HierarchyStateChanged
member.summary: Updates the enabled state based on changes in the hierarchy. OnEnable/OnDisable are only called if the GameObject is in an active Scene.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.CanDestroy
member.summary: Checks if this MonoBehaviour can be destroyed.
member.returns: True if the MonoBehaviour can be destroyed, false otherwise.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.OnAddedToScene
member.summary: Called when the GameObject is added to a Scene. This happens before OnEnable and only once per scene add.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.OnRemovedFromScene
member.summary: Called when the GameObject is removed from a Scene. This happens after OnDisable and only once per scene remove.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.OnEnable
member.summary: Called when the object becomes enabled and active. Only called when the GameObject is in a Scene and enabled.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.OnDisable
member.summary: Called when the object becomes disabled or inactive. Only called when the GameObject was in a Scene and becomes disabled.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.Start
member.summary: Called before the first frame update.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.FixedUpdate
member.summary: Called at fixed time intervals.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.Update
member.summary: Called every frame.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.LateUpdate
member.summary: Called every frame after all Update functions have been called.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.OnRenderCollect(Prowl.Runtime.Camera,System.Collections.Generic.List{Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.IRenderable},System.Collections.Generic.List{Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.IRenderableLight})
member.summary: Called every frame per camera to collect render data. Always called regardless of play mode. Components add their renderables/lights to the provided lists. Camera is provided for LOD and distance-based decisions.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.DrawGizmos
member.summary: Called for rendering and handling GUI gizmos.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.OnGui(Prowl.PaperUI.Paper)
member.summary: Called for drawing and handling interaction with Runtime/Ingame UI

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.InternalStart
member.summary: Gated Start — only runs in play mode or with [ExecuteAlways].

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.InternalUpdate
member.summary: Gated Update — only runs in play mode or with [ExecuteAlways].

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.InternalLateUpdate
member.summary: Gated LateUpdate — only runs in play mode or with [ExecuteAlways].

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.InternalFixedUpdate
member.summary: Gated FixedUpdate — only runs in play mode or with [ExecuteAlways].

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.InternalOnEnable
member.summary: Gated OnEnable — only runs in play mode or with [ExecuteAlways].

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.InternalOnDisable
member.summary: Gated OnDisable — only runs in play mode or with [ExecuteAlways].

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.MonoBehaviour.OnDispose
member.summary: Called when the MonoBehaviour will be destroyed. This is an override of EngineObject.OnDispose() and is also exposed as a virtual lifecycle method.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.TagLayerManager
member.summary: Manages tags and layers for GameObjects in the Prowl Game Engine. This class is a ScriptableSingleton, ensuring only one instance exists. The tags and layers data is stored in a file named "TagAndLayers.setting".

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.TagLayerManager.tags
member.summary: List of available tags for GameObjects.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.TagLayerManager.layers
member.summary: Array of available layers for GameObjects.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.TagLayerManager.GetTag(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves the tag name associated with the given index.
member.param: The index of the tag to retrieve.
member.returns: The tag name at the specified index, or "Untagged" if the index is out of range.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.TagLayerManager.GetLayer(System.Int32)
member.summary: Retrieves the layer name associated with the given index.
member.param: The index of the layer to retrieve.
member.returns: The layer name at the specified index, "Default" If index is out of range.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.TagLayerManager.GetTagIndex(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the index of the specified tag.
member.param: The tag name to look up.
member.returns: The index of the tag, or -1 if the tag is not found.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.TagLayerManager.GetLayerIndex(System.String)
member.summary: Retrieves the index of the specified layer.
member.param: The layer name to look up.
member.returns: The index of the layer, or -1 if the layer is not found.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.TagLayerManager.GetLayers
member.summary: Retrieves a copy of the layers array.
member.returns: A new array containing all layer names.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Graphics.ResetState
member.summary: Resets GL rasterizer state to known defaults and syncs the cache. Call at the start of each pipeline Render to prevent stale state from a previous render pass leaking in.

member.summary: Applies an exponential curve to the input for more precise control at low values.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.InputActionType
member.summary: The type of input action determines how the action processes input.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputActionType.Value
member.summary: The action continuously provides a value based on the input. Examples: movement axes, mouse position, analog stick

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputActionType.Button
member.summary: The action acts like a button with discrete press and release events. Examples: jump, fire, interact buttons

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputActionType.PassThrough
member.summary: The action passes through all input events without filtering. Useful for low-level input handling where you need every value change.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.InputActionPhase
member.summary: The phase of an input action indicates its current state.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputActionPhase.Disabled
member.summary: The action is disabled and not listening to input.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputActionPhase.Waiting
member.summary: The action is enabled and waiting for input.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputActionPhase.Started
member.summary: The action has been activated and is starting.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputActionPhase.Performed
member.summary: The action is performing (processing input).

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputActionPhase.Canceled
member.summary: The action was canceled before completing.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.InputInteractionType
member.summary: Defines how a control must be actuated to trigger an action.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputInteractionType.Default
member.summary: Default behavior - triggers when control is actuated. Performs immediately on press, continues while held.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputInteractionType.Press
member.summary: Triggers only on the initial press (down). Does not continue to perform while held.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputInteractionType.Release
member.summary: Triggers only when the control is released (up).

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputInteractionType.Hold
member.summary: Requires the control to be held for a specified duration before triggering. Performs once after the hold duration is met.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputInteractionType.MultiTap
member.summary: Requires multiple rapid presses (taps) to trigger. Must be pressed N times within a time window.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputInteractionType.PressAndRelease
member.summary: Triggers on both press and release as separate events. Useful for actions that need distinct down/up handling.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.InputInteractionType.Tap
member.summary: Triggers only when the control is pressed quickly and released. Cancels if held too long.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.InteractionState
member.summary: Internal state tracker for interaction logic.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.InputBindingType
member.summary: Defines what type of input control a binding refers to.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.InputBinding
member.summary: Represents a binding from an input control to an action. A binding connects a physical input (like a key or button) to a logical action.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.InputBinding.BindingType
member.summary: The type of input control this binding refers to.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.InputBinding.Key
member.summary: The specific key if this is a keyboard binding.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.InputBinding.MouseButton
member.summary: The specific mouse button if this is a mouse button binding.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.InputBinding.GamepadButton
member.summary: The specific gamepad button if this is a gamepad binding.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.InputBinding.AxisIndex
member.summary: The axis index if this is an axis binding.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.InputBinding.Interaction
member.summary: Optional interaction that determines how this binding triggers the action.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.InputBinding.HoldDuration
member.summary: Duration (in seconds) required for Hold interaction. Default: 0.4s

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.InputBinding.TapCount
member.summary: Number of taps required for MultiTap interaction. Default: 2

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.InputBinding.TapWindow
member.summary: Maximum time window (in seconds) between taps for MultiTap. Default: 0.3s

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.InputBinding.MaxTapDuration
member.summary: Maximum duration (in seconds) for a Tap to be valid. Default: 0.2s

member.summary: The component bindings that make up this composite.

member.summary: Optional processors to apply to the final composite value. These are applied after combining the individual parts.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Vector2CompositeBinding
member.summary: Combines four buttons (up, down, left, right) into a Vector2. Commonly used for WASD or arrow key movement.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Vector2CompositeBinding.Normalize
member.summary: Whether to normalize the resulting vector to have a maximum magnitude of 1. Useful to prevent diagonal movement from being faster.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.DualAxisCompositeBinding
member.summary: Combines two axes into a Vector2. Commonly used for analog sticks or mouse delta.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.AxisCompositeBinding
member.summary: Combines positive and negative buttons into a single axis value. Example: A/D keys for horizontal movement (-1 to 1).

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.NullInputHandler
member.summary: A no-op input handler that returns safe defaults for all queries. Used as a fallback when no real input handler is on the stack.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.InstancedMeshRenderable
member.summary: A simple instanced renderable for drawing multiple instances of a mesh. Useful for drawing many copies of the same object efficiently (trees, grass, particles, etc.) Uses the mesh's cached instance VAO for optimal performance.

member.summary: Clears all filters from the chain.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.CompositeBroadPhaseFilter.Filter(Jitter2.Collision.IDynamicTreeProxy,Jitter2.Collision.IDynamicTreeProxy)
member.summary: Filters the collision by running all registered filters. Returns true only if all filters return true. If any filter returns false, processing stops immediately (short-circuit evaluation).

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.RaycastHit
member.summary: Contains information about a raycast hit.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.RaycastHit.Hit
member.summary: If the ray hit something.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.RaycastHit.Distance
member.summary: The distance from the ray's origin to the impact point.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.RaycastHit.Normal
member.summary: The normal of the surface the ray hit.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.RaycastHit.Point
member.summary: The point in world space where the ray hit the collider.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.RaycastHit.Rigidbody
member.summary: The Rigidbody3D of the collider that was hit.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.RaycastHit.Shape
member.summary: The Shape that was hit.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.RaycastHit.Transform
member.summary: The Transform of the rigidbody that was hit.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.ShapeCastHit
member.summary: Contains information about a shape cast hit.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.ShapeCastHit.Hit
member.summary: If the shape cast hit something.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.ShapeCastHit.Fraction
member.summary: The fraction/lambda along the sweep direction where the hit occurred (0 = start, 1 = end of sweep). Note: This is not a distance, but a normalized value between 0 and 1.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.ShapeCastHit.Penetration
member.summary: The amount of penetration at the hit point. Only valid for overlap casts.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.ShapeCastHit.Normal
member.summary: The normal of the surface the shape hit.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.ShapeCastHit.Point
member.summary: The point in world space on the casting shape where it hit the collider (at t=0).

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.ShapeCastHit.HitPoint
member.summary: The point in world space on the hit collider where the cast shape touched it (at t=0).

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.ShapeCastHit.Rigidbody
member.summary: The Rigidbody3D of the collider that was hit.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.ShapeCastHit.Shape
member.summary: The Shape that was hit.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.ShapeCastHit.Transform
member.summary: The Transform of the rigidbody that was hit.

member.summary: Instantiate a prefab from an AssetRef.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.PropertyOverride
member.summary: Stores a single per-instance property override for a prefab instance.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.PropertyOverride.Path
member.summary: Path to the overridden field. Format: "{componentIdentifier}.{fieldPath}" for component fields, or "$.{fieldName}" for GameObject-level fields.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.PropertyOverride.Value
member.summary: The overridden value, serialized as an EchoObject.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.BRDFLutGenerator
member.summary: Generates a 2D BRDF Integration LUT for split-sum IBL approximation. The LUT is indexed by (NdotV, roughness) and stores (scale, bias) for the Fresnel split-sum: F0 * scale + bias. This is pre-computed once and set as a global texture "_BRDFLut".

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.BRDFLutGenerator.GetLut
member.summary: Get or generate the BRDF LUT texture.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.BRDFLutGenerator.UploadGlobal
member.summary: Upload the BRDF LUT as a global texture so all shaders can access it. Call once at startup or when the LUT is invalidated.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.DefaultRenderPipeline
member.summary: Default rendering pipeline implementation that handles standard forward rendering, post-processing effects, shadows, and debug visualization.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ForwardLightData
member.summary: Shadow data exposed by a light for the forward pipeline.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ForwardLightManager
member.summary: Selects the most relevant lights for the frame and uploads their data as global uniforms for forward rendering shaders.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ForwardLightManager.SelectAndUploadLights(Prowl.Vector.Float3,System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.IRenderableLight},Prowl.Runtime.LayerMask)
member.summary: Select up to 8 lights and upload their uniform data for forward shaders. Directional light always goes to index 0. Point/spot sorted by distance to camera.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GlobalUniformsData
member.summary: Structure matching the layout of global uniforms in ShaderVariables.glsl Uses std140 layout for uniform buffer compatibility Contains only per-frame data that is constant across all draw calls

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GlobalUniforms
member.summary: Manages the global uniform buffer for efficient shader data upload

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GlobalUniforms.Initialize
member.summary: Initializes the global uniform buffer

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GlobalUniforms.Upload
member.summary: Updates the GPU buffer if data has changed

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GlobalUniforms.GetBuffer
member.summary: Gets the uniform buffer for binding to shaders

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GlobalUniforms.Dispose
member.summary: Cleans up the global uniform buffer resources

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GlobalUniforms.Clear
member.summary: Resets all data to defaults

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.BloomEffect
member.summary: Bloom effect using dual filtering (downsample + upsample) for fast, high-quality blur. Much faster than separable Gaussian or Kawase ping-pong at equivalent quality.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.BloomEffect.Intensity
member.summary: Bloom intensity multiplier.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.BloomEffect.Threshold
member.summary: Luminance threshold for bright pixel extraction.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.BloomEffect.Iterations
member.summary: Number of downsample iterations. More = wider bloom but more GPU cost. 4-8 is typical.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GTAOEffect
member.summary: Ground-Truth Ambient Occlusion (GTAO) effect for realistic ambient occlusion. Based on "Practical Realtime Strategies for Accurate Indirect Occlusion" by Activision. Reference: https://www.activision.com/cdn/research/Practical_Real_Time_Strategies_for_Accurate_Indirect_Occlusion_NEW%20VERSION_COLOR.pdf

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GTAOEffect.Slices
member.summary: Number of angular slices to sample around each pixel. Higher = better quality but slower.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GTAOEffect.DirectionSamples
member.summary: Number of samples per direction. Higher = better quality but slower.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GTAOEffect.Radius
member.summary: Sampling radius in world space units. Larger = more prominent occlusion from distant objects.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GTAOEffect.Intensity
member.summary: Overall intensity of the ambient occlusion effect. Higher = darker occlusion.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GTAOEffect.BlurRadius
member.summary: Blur radius for denoising. 0 = no blur (noisy but sharp), higher = smoother but less detailed.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.GTAOEffect.ResolutionScale
member.summary: Resolution scale for AO calculation. 1.0 = full resolution, 0.5 = half resolution (better performance).

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ScreenSpaceReflectionEffect
member.summary: Screen Space Reflections (SSR) effect that renders realistic reflections using the visible geometry on screen. Works best on smooth, metallic surfaces and automatically fades on rough surfaces.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ScreenSpaceReflectionEffect.MaxSteps
member.summary: Maximum number of steps to march along a ray. Higher = better quality but slower.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ScreenSpaceReflectionEffect.BinarySearchIterations
member.summary: Number of binary search iterations to refine ray hit position. 0 = no refinement.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ScreenSpaceReflectionEffect.Intensity
member.summary: Overall intensity of reflections. 0 = no reflections, 1 = full strength.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ScreenSpaceReflectionEffect.ScreenEdgeFade
member.summary: How much reflections fade near screen edges. Higher = sharper fade.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ScreenSpaceReflectionEffect.MipBias
member.summary: Mip bias when sampling reflection color. Higher = blurrier reflections.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ScreenSpaceReflectionEffect.BlurRadius
member.summary: Blur radius applied to reflections. Higher = softer reflections.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ScreenSpaceReflectionEffect.ResolutionScale
member.summary: Resolution scale for ray marching. 0.5 = half resolution (better performance).

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.InstanceData
member.summary: Per-instance data for GPU instanced rendering. This struct is uploaded to a GPU buffer for instanced draw calls. Layout matches standard shader instance attributes.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.InstanceData.#ctor(Prowl.Vector.Float4x4)
member.summary: Creates instance data from a transform matrix.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.InstanceData.#ctor(Prowl.Vector.Float4x4,Prowl.Vector.Color)
member.summary: Creates instance data from a transform matrix and color as Float4.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.InstanceData.#ctor(Prowl.Vector.Float4x4,Prowl.Vector.Color,Prowl.Vector.Float4)
member.summary: Creates instance data with all properties.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.InstanceData.GetMatrix
member.summary: Gets the transform matrix from this instance data.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.InstanceData.SizeInBytes
member.summary: Size of the instance data structure in bytes.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.PropertyState.ComputeHash
member.summary: Computes a FNV-1a 64-bit hash representing the current state of all properties. Used for material batching to group objects with identical material properties together, minimizing GPU state changes. Properties are ordered by key to ensure consistent hashing.
member.returns: A 64-bit FNV-1a hash of all property key-value pairs

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.PropertyState.ApplyMaterialUniforms(Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.PropertyState,Prowl.Runtime.GraphicsProgram,System.Int32@)
member.summary:
Applies material-level uniforms to the shader (called ONCE per material batch). Material uniforms are properties shared by all objects using the same material (e.g., albedo color, roughness, normal maps). This method uses the uniform cache to avoid redundant GPU calls when values haven't changed.
Batching flow: Global uniforms → Material uniforms → Per-object (instance) uniforms
member.param: The material's property state
The compiled shader program to bind uniforms to
Texture slot counter, incremented as textures are bound

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.PropertyState.ApplyInstanceUniforms(Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.PropertyState,Prowl.Runtime.GraphicsProgram,System.Int32@)
member.summary:
Applies per-object (instance) uniforms to the shader (called ONCE per draw call). Instance uniforms are properties unique to each object (e.g., tint color, instance ID, bone matrices for skinning). These can override material properties if they share the same name. Uses the uniform cache to skip redundant GPU calls.
Batching flow: Global uniforms → Material uniforms → Per-object (instance) uniforms
member.param: The instance's property state (per-object overrides)
The compiled shader program to bind uniforms to
Texture slot counter, continues from where material textures left off

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderContext
member.summary: Provides access to rendering resources for image effects. Passed to effects during rendering to give access to all necessary buffers and state.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderContext.DepthNormals
member.summary: Depth + normals from the pre-pass. InternalDepth = depth, InternalTextures[0] = view-space normals.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderContext.SceneColor
member.summary: The scene color buffer (forward-rendered opaques + skybox).

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderContext.ReplaceSceneColor(Prowl.Runtime.Resources.RenderTexture)
member.summary: Replaces the scene color buffer with a new one (e.g., for HDR to LDR conversion). Only allowed during PostProcess stage.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderingData.DisplayGizmos
member.summary: Whether to draw gizmos (editor scene view).

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderingData.DisplayGrid
member.summary: Whether to draw the editor grid.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.IRenderable
member.summary: Interface for all renderable objects in the scene. Supports both single-instance and GPU-instanced rendering through a unified API.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.IRenderable.GetPosition
member.summary: Gets the world-space position of this renderable (typically the transform position). Used for depth sorting (e.g., back-to-front sorting for transparent objects).

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.IRenderable.GetSubMeshIndex
member.summary: Gets the submesh index to draw. -1 means draw the entire index buffer (no submeshes).

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.IRenderable.GetRenderingData(Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ViewerData,Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.PropertyState@,Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Mesh@,Prowl.Vector.Float4x4@,Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.InstanceData[]@)
member.summary: Gets the rendering data for this renderable.
member.param: Camera viewing data for culling/LOD
Shader properties (per-object or shared for instances)
Mesh to render
Model matrix (only used for single-instance rendering)
Instance data array for GPU instancing, or null for single-instance rendering

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.IRenderableLight.GetForwardLightData
member.summary: Returns the light's data for forward rendering (position, color, shadow data, etc.)

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderPipeline.CollectRenderables(Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Scene,Prowl.Runtime.Camera)
member.summary: Collects renderables and lights from the scene for the given camera. Components receive camera info for LOD/culling decisions.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderPipeline.SortRenderables(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.IRenderable},System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Int32},Prowl.Vector.Float3,Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderPipeline.SortMode)
member.summary: Sorts renderables by distance from camera and returns a new sorted list. FrontToBack: Nearest objects first (optimal for opaque objects - early Z rejection) BackToFront: Farthest objects first (required for transparent objects - correct alpha blending)

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderPipeline.DrawMeshNow(Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Mesh,Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Material,System.Int32)
member.summary: Immediately draws a mesh without queuing. Used internally by the render pipeline. For queued rendering, use Graphics.DrawMesh() instead.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderPipeline.RenderBatch
member.summary: Represents a render batch: a group of objects sharing the same material, mesh, and shader pass. Batching reduces GPU state changes by binding material uniforms once for all objects in the batch.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderPipeline.DrawRenderables(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyList{Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.IRenderable},System.String,System.String,Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ViewerData,System.Collections.Generic.HashSet{System.Int32},System.Boolean)
member.summary:
Renders all given objects with optimized batching. Objects are grouped by (material, mesh, pass) to minimize GPU state changes. This achieves: - Material uniforms bound once per batch (instead of per object) - Mesh data uploaded once per batch - Shader variant selected once per batch - Per-object uniforms still bound individually
Performance: 100 objects with same material = 1 material bind (vs 100 without batching)

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderPipeline.DrawInstancedRenderablePass(Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.IRenderable,Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Material,Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Mesh,System.Int32,Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.ViewerData)
member.summary: Draws a specific pass of an instanced renderable with GPU instancing. Uses DrawIndexedInstanced to draw multiple instances in a single draw call. The mesh's cached VAO system is used for optimal performance.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderStage
member.summary: Defines the stages in the forward rendering pipeline where image effects can be injected.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderStage.AfterOpaques
member.summary: Runs after opaque rendering but before transparent objects. Has access to depth, normals, and the opaque scene color. Perfect for SSR, GTAO, screen-space effects that need opaque geometry.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.RenderStage.PostProcess
member.summary: Runs after all rendering (including transparents) as final post-processing. Perfect for tonemapping, color grading, bloom, DOF, FXAA, etc.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.Shaders.BlendDescription
member.summary: A component describing the blend behavior for an individual shader pass.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.Shaders.BlendDescription.BlendEnabled
member.summary: Controls whether blending is enabled for the shader pass.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.Shaders.BlendDescription.SourceColorFactor
member.summary: Controls the source color's influence on the blend result.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.Shaders.BlendDescription.DestinationColorFactor
member.summary: Controls the destination color's influence on the blend result.

member.summary: Controls the function used to combine the source and destination color factors.

member.summary: Controls the source alpha's influence on the blend result.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.Shaders.BlendDescription.DestinationAlphaFactor
member.summary: Controls the destination alpha's influence on the blend result.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.Shaders.BlendDescription.AlphaFunction
member.summary: Controls the function used to combine the source and destination alpha factors.
Constructs a new .
member.param: Controls whether blending is enabled for the color attachment.
Controls the source color's influence on the blend result.
Controls the destination color's influence on the blend result.
Controls the function used to combine the source and destination color factors.
Controls the source alpha's influence on the blend result.
Controls the destination alpha's influence on the blend result.
Controls the function used to combine the source and destination alpha factors.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.Shaders.BlendDescription.OverrideBlend
member.summary: Describes a blend attachment state in which the source completely overrides the destination. Settings: BlendEnabled = true ColorWriteMask = null SourceColorFactor = BlendFactor.One DestinationColorFactor = BlendFactor.Zero ColorFunction = BlendFunction.Add SourceAlphaFactor = BlendFactor.One DestinationAlphaFactor = BlendFactor.Zero AlphaFunction = BlendFunction.Add

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.Shaders.BlendDescription.AlphaBlend
member.summary: Describes a blend attachment state in which the source and destination are blended in an inverse relationship. Settings: BlendEnabled = true ColorWriteMask = null SourceColorFactor = BlendFactor.SourceAlpha DestinationColorFactor = BlendFactor.InverseSourceAlpha ColorFunction = BlendFunction.Add SourceAlphaFactor = BlendFactor.SourceAlpha DestinationAlphaFactor = BlendFactor.InverseSourceAlpha AlphaFunction = BlendFunction.Add

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.Shaders.BlendDescription.AdditiveBlend
member.summary: Describes a blend attachment state in which the source is added to the destination based on its alpha channel. Settings: BlendEnabled = true ColorWriteMask = null SourceColorFactor = BlendFactor.SourceAlpha DestinationColorFactor = BlendFactor.One ColorFunction = BlendFunction.Add SourceAlphaFactor = BlendFactor.SourceAlpha DestinationAlphaFactor = BlendFactor.One AlphaFunction = BlendFunction.Add

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Rendering.Shaders.BlendDescription.Disabled
member.summary: Describes a blend attachment state in which blending is disabled. Settings: BlendEnabled = false ColorWriteMask = null SourceColorFactor = BlendFactor.One DestinationColorFactor = BlendFactor.Zero ColorFunction = BlendFunction.Add SourceAlphaFactor = BlendFactor.One DestinationAlphaFactor = BlendFactor.Zero AlphaFunction = BlendFunction.Add

member.summary: Element-wise equality.

member.returns: True if all elements and all array elements are equal; false otherswise.

member.summary: Per-bone animation data with separate curves for each transform component.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.AudioClip
member.summary: Represents audio data that can be played back or streamed by an AudioSource. Supported file types are WAV/MP3/FlAC/OGG.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.AudioClip.FilePath
member.summary: If the constructor with 'string filePath' overloaded is used this will contain the file path, or string.Empty otherwise.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.AudioClip.Name
member.summary: The name of this AudioClip. If the filepath constructor is used it will contain the filepath, otherwise the string is empty.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.AudioClip.StreamFromDisk
member.summary: If true, data will be streamed from disk. This is useful when a sound is longer than just a couple of seconds. If data is loaded from memory, this property has no effect.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.AudioClip.Handle
member.summary: If the constructor with 'byte[] data' overload is used this will contain a pointer to the allocated memory of the data. Do not manually free!

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.AudioClip.Hash
member.summary: Gets the hash code used to identify the data of this AudioClip. Only applicable if the 'byte[] data' overload is used.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.AudioClip.DataSize
member.summary: If the constructor with 'byte[] data' overload is used this will contain the size of the data in number of bytes.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.AudioClip.#ctor(System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new AudioClip instance which gets its data from a file on disk. The file must be in an encoded format.
member.param: The filepath of the encoded audio file (WAV/MP3/FLAC/OGG)
If true, streams data from disk rather than loading the entire file into memory for playback. Typically you'd stream from disk if a sound is more than just a couple of seconds long.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.AudioClip.#ctor(System.Byte[],System.Boolean)
member.summary: Creates a new AudioClip instance which gets its data from memory. The data must be in an encoded format.
member.param: Must be encoded audio data (either WAV/MP3/FLAC/OGG)
If true, then this clip will not use shared memory. If true, this clip will reuse existing memory if possible.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Material.GetStateHash
member.summary: Gets a hash representing the current material state (uniform values only, not keywords or shader). The hash is used by the renderer to batch objects with identical material properties together, minimizing GPU uniform binding overhead. The hash is cached and only recalculated when dirty.
member.returns: A 64-bit hash of all material uniform values

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Material.MarkDirty
member.summary: Marks the material as dirty, forcing a hash recalculation on next GetStateHash() call. Called automatically when any material property is modified.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Material.SyncShaderDefaults
member.summary: Fills in any missing properties from the shader's declared defaults. Does NOT overwrite existing values — only adds properties that are absent.

member.summary: Serialized GO hierarchy.

member.summary: The serialized GameObject hierarchy in Echo format.

member.name: P:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Scene.Current
member.summary: The currently active scene managed by the built-in Scene Manager. For simple games, use Scene.Load() and Scene.Current for automatic scene management. For advanced use cases (e.g., multiplayer servers with multiple scenes), create and manage your own Scene instances directly.

member.name: E:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Scene.OnSceneLoaded
member.summary: Fires after a scene is loaded via Load().

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Scene.Load(Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Scene)
member.summary: Loads a scene as the current active scene, replacing any previously loaded scene. The previous scene will be disabled and disposed.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Scene.LoadWithoutEnable(Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Scene)
member.summary: Unloads the current scene, disabling and disposing it. After calling this, Scene.Current will be null.
Loads a scene as Current without calling Enable(). Kept for backward compatibility — now just calls Load() since lifecycle gating is handled per-component via ShouldExecuteGameplay.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Scene._goIdentifiers
member.summary: Parallel to serializeObj — stores the original identifier for each GO.

member.name: F:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Scene._compIdentifiers
member.summary: Flat array of component identifiers. _compIdOffsets[i] is the index into this array for GO i's first component. Component count = offset[i+1] - offset[i].

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Scene.Disable

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.Resources.Scene.Update
member.summary: Updates all active GameObjects and their components in this scene. PreUpdate handles Start (gated by ShouldExecuteGameplay). Update and LateUpdate are gated internally by each component's ShouldExecuteGameplay.

member.summary: Check if a GUID corresponds to a built-in asset.

member.summary: Get the deterministic GUID for a specific default model.

member.summary: Get the deterministic GUID for a default model's first mesh.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.GameResources
member.summary: Load assets by path at runtime. Assets under any Resources/ folder in the project are always included in builds and can be loaded by their relative path. Example: Assets/Art/Resources/Textures/Grass.png → GameResources.Load<Texture2D>("Textures/Grass")

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.GameResources.Initialize(System.Collections.Generic.Dictionary{System.String,System.Guid})
member.summary: Initialize the resources mapping. Called by the player at startup.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.GameResources.Load``1(System.String)
member.summary: Load an asset by its resource path (relative to the nearest Resources/ folder, no extension). Returns null if the path is not found or the asset can't be loaded.

member.summary: Check if a resource path exists.

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.GameResources.GetGuid(System.String)
member.summary: Get the GUID for a resource path, or Guid.Empty if not found.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.SkinnedMeshRenderable
member.summary: A renderable for skinned meshes that uses pre-computed world-space bounds (derived from bone positions) instead of the static mesh bind-pose bounds. This ensures correct frustum culling for animated/deformed meshes.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.Utils.TinyMathParser
member.summary:
A simple math parser that can evaluate basic arithmetic expressions. Supports addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, and exponentiation. Also supports parentheses and variables, and applies the C operator precedence.
Notes: Ignores whitespace characters. Variables are case-sensitive. Variables must be defined in the Variables dictionary before parsing.

member.name: T:Prowl.Runtime.MultiValueDictionary`2
member.summary:
A MultiValueDictionary can be viewed as a that allows multiple values for any given unique key. While the MultiValueDictionary API is mostly the same as that of a regular , there is a distinction in that getting the value for a key returns a of values rather than a single value associated with that key. Additionally, there is functionality to allow adding or removing more than a single value at once.
The MultiValueDictionary can also be viewed as a IReadOnlyDictionary<TKey,IReadOnlyCollection<TValue>t> where the is abstracted from the view of the programmer.
For a read-only MultiValueDictionary, see .
member.typeparam: The type of the key.
The type of the value.

members.member:
Returns whether the specified type is a primitive, enum, string, decimal, or struct that consists only of those types, allowing to do a deep-copy by simply assigning it.
Source: https://github.com/AdamsLair/duality/blob/4156e696b381e508df409ca4741d1eae88223287/Source/Core/Duality/Utility/ReflectionHelper.cs#L470

Returns a TypeInfos BaseType as a TypeInfo, or null if it was null.
Source: https://github.com/AdamsLair/duality/blob/4156e696b381e508df409ca4741d1eae88223287/Source/Core/Duality/Utility/Extensions/ExtMethodsTypeInfo.cs#L45

Returns a Types inheritance level. The object-Type has an inheritance level of zero, each subsequent inheritance increases it by one.
Source: https://github.com/AdamsLair/duality/blob/4156e696b381e508df409ca4741d1eae88223287/Source/Core/Duality/Utility/Extensions/ExtMethodsTypeInfo.cs#L45

Returns all fields that are declared within this Type, or any of its base Types. Includes public, non-public, static and instance fields.
Source: https://github.com/AdamsLair/duality/blob/4156e696b381e508df409ca4741d1eae88223287/Source/Core/Duality/Utility/Extensions/ExtMethodsTypeInfo.cs#L45

Returns all properties that are declared within this Type, or any of its base Types. Includes public, non-public, static and instance properties.
Source: https://github.com/AdamsLair/duality/blob/4156e696b381e508df409ca4741d1eae88223287/Source/Core/Duality/Utility/Extensions/ExtMethodsTypeInfo.cs#L45

Returns all members that are declared within this Type, or any of its base Types. Includes public, non-public, static and instance fields.
Source: https://github.com/AdamsLair/duality/blob/4156e696b381e508df409ca4741d1eae88223287/Source/Core/Duality/Utility/Extensions/ExtMethodsTypeInfo.cs#L45

member.name: M:Prowl.Runtime.RuntimeUtils.AsInt(System.Boolean)
member.summary: An ultra-fast way to convert a bool to an int.

member.name: M:Prowl.Vector.Transform.GetRelativePath(Prowl.Vector.Transform,Prowl.Vector.Transform)
member.summary: Gets the path from root to target, excluding root's own name. E.g. if root is "Model" and target is "Model/Armature/Hips/Spine", returns "Armature/Hips/Spine". Compatible with Transform.Find().

member.name: T:Prowl.Echo.GenerateSerializerAttribute
member.summary: Marks a class or struct for automatic ISerializable implementation via source generation. The generator will create optimized Serialize and Deserialize methods based on the type's fields.

member.name: T:RequireComponentAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that a GameObject must have certain components in addition to the one where this attribute is applied.
member.remarks:
When this attribute is used on a component, the Prowl Game Engine will automatically add the required components to the GameObject if they are not already present. This ensures that the component has all its dependencies met.
This attribute can only be applied to classes (typically components deriving from MonoBehaviour).

member.name: P:RequireComponentAttribute.types
member.summary: Gets the array of component types that are required by this component.

member.name: M:RequireComponentAttribute.#ctor(System.Type[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the RequireComponentAttribute class.
member.param: An array of Type objects representing the required components.
